title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels arrived in Boston Harbor for the six-day Sail Boston 2026 event.
The USS Constitution, leading the Parade of Sail, fired a cannon as it docked at Charlestown Navy Yard.
The event, showcasing ships from over 20 countries, attracted thousands of visitors to celebrate Boston’s maritime history.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox select Jake Schaffner as the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th by MLB.com, played shortstop for University of North Carolina.
In 2026, Schaffner recorded a .356/.467/.552 slash line with six home runs and 50 RBIs.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Grand Parade of Sail featured historic vessels, led by the USS Constitution, attracting spectators from Castle Island to Piers Park.
Chris Hood, a shipbuilder, emphasized the historical significance of clipper ships, likening them to modern freight services.
The event featured vessels like the Bluenose Two and Schooner Adventure, celebrating Boston's 250th maritime anniversary.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello recorded a 10.35 ERA in eight starts for Boston Red Sox before being demoted to Triple-A Worcester.
Bello was named the midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic due to his poor pitching performance.
Despite a successful relief stint with a 0.71 ERA, Bello remains in Triple-A as the Red Sox opted for Eduardo Rivera.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox on a seven-game winning streak, just two games behind the final American League wild-card spot.
Boston management, led by Craig Breslow, faces decision to buy or sell assets before 2026 MLB trade deadline.
Team's recent rebound challenges previous plans to sell, complicating strategy at the trade deadline.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden on July 10, 2026, as part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour.
The concert featured a mix of nostalgia and new music, including hits like 'Hips Don't Lie' and 'Waka Waka'.
Shakira's setlist included tributes to her Colombian roots and personal songs reflecting her recent life challenges.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Healey announced $18.6M in tax credits to build 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects involve converting historic buildings into apartments in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester.
Funding is part of the Housing Development Incentive Program to tackle high housing costs and shortages.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, arrested in Albany, NY, for bank robberies in Nashua, NH, and Boonton, NJ.
Sawyer allegedly used a semiautomatic handgun to steal $6,000 in Nashua and $12,000 in Boonton.
The FBI's Boston-led Operation Summer Heat 2.0 involved multi-state agencies in Sawyer's arrest.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, found dead near his motorcycle off Route 2 in Shelburne, NH, after a week-long search.
Grondin was last seen on July 4; a concerned resident discovered his body on Thursday night.
Preliminary investigation shows Grondin crossed lanes and hit a post; no other vehicle involved in the crash.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 as 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts for his contributions to music and mental health advocacy.
Noah Kahan is the first artist to headline four consecutive sold-out concerts at Fenway Park, with over 150,000 fans attending.
Kahan, originally from Vermont, has strong ties to Massachusetts, highlighted in his music and advocacy through the Busyhead Project.
